channel.title: Howto on Have an average day :|
channel.description: Recent content in Howto on Have an average day :|

item.title: How to mail enable an Active Directory Group on Exchange 2010
item.description: I needed to take a security group and convert it turn it into a distribution group. This is actually pretty simple. First edit the group and change it to a distribution group. Make sure it’s a universal group, only universal groups can be mail enabled. Give it an email address, make sure the membership is correct and move on to your exchange console. Go to distribution Groups Right Click New distribution Group Existing Account Done Now you have mail enabled your group.

item.title: System Sweeper PXE Boot.
item.description: As you may know, Microsoft released a standalone system sweeper for malware removal, http://connect.microsoft.com/systemsweeper. This allows you to create a CD/DVD, UFD, or ISO. While this works well I wanted to boot this via PXE. I hate having to find media, set it up, etc. I’d rather just use WDS to boot a WIM and be done with it. So I created some media and extracted the files needed for the system scanner and created my own System Sweeper WIM to boot via PXE.

item.title: MediaWiki
item.description: Well I got MediaWiki Setup to help get our documentation here at work up to snuff with the rest of the world. We are using ton of folders with a bunch of word docs, pdfs, txt, etc. to keep our information. I’ve been meaning to do this for a while but I haven’t been able to get it done. So I stat down and installed and and got LDAP authentications setup.
